{{Short description|Czechoslovak-born AI researcher (born 1986)}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2018}}
{{Infobox scientist
| name              = Andrej Karpathy
| | image             = Andrej Karpathy, OpenAI.png <!--(filename only, i.e. without "File:" prefix)-->
| | image_size        = 
| | caption           = Karpathy at Stanford in 2016
| | birth_name        = Andrej Karpathy
| | birth_date        = {{Birth date and age|df=yes|1986|10|23}}
| | birth_place       = [[Bratislava]], [[Czechoslovakia]] (now Slovakia) 
| | fields            = [[Machine learning]]<br>[[Computer vision]]<br>[[Artificial intelligence]]<ref name=gs/>
| | workplaces        = {{Plainlist|
| * [[OpenAI]] (2015–2017, 2023-2024)
| * [[Tesla, Inc.]] (2017–2022)}}
| | alma_mater        = {{Plainlist|
| * [[University of Toronto]] ([[Bachelor of Science|BS]])
| * [[University of British Columbia]] ([[Master of Science|MS]])
| * [[Stanford University]] ([[Doctor of Philosophy|PhD]])}}
| | awards            = [[Innovators Under 35]] (2020)
| TIME100 Most Influential People in AI (2024)<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://time.com/7012851/andrej-karpathy/ |title=U of T CS alumni among Time magazine's TIME100 Most Influential People in AI |access-date=25 June 2025 |archive-date=21 June 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250621123152/https://time.com/7012851/andrej-karpathy/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
| | thesis_title      = Connecting Images and Natural Language
| | thesis_url        = https://searchworks.stanford.edu/view/11849345
| | thesis_year       = 2016
| | doctoral_advisor  = [[Fei-Fei Li]]
| | website           = {{Official URL}}
|}}

'''Andrej Karpathy''' (born 23 October 1986<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://twitter.com/karpathy/status/261161354969358337 |title=Self-reported on twitter |access-date=25 April 2019 |archive-date=23 August 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210823163529/https://twitter.com/karpathy/status/261161354969358337 |url-status=live }}</ref>) is a Slovak-Canadian AI researcher<ref name=":3" />, who co-founded and formerly worked at [[OpenAI]],<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.autonews.com/article/20180426/ANE/180429844/tesla-s-autopilot-chief-steps-down-after-two-years/ |title=Tesla's Autopilot chief steps down after two years|date=26 April 2018|accessdate=9 August 2018|work=Automotive News Europe}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2017/11/06/technology/artificial-intelligence-start-up.html |title=A.I. Researchers Leave Elon Musk Lab to Begin Robotics Start-Up |work=The New York Times |date=7 November 2017 |accessdate=9 August 2018 |last1=Metz |first1=Cade |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707161547/https://www.nytimes.com/2017/11/06/technology/artificial-intelligence-start-up.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2018/04/19/technology/artificial-intelligence-salaries-openai.html |title=A.I. Researchers Are Making More Than $1 Million, Even at a Nonprofit |work=The New York Times |date=19 April 2017 |accessdate=9 August 2018 |last1=Metz |first1=Cade |archive-date=8 August 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180808200610/https://www.nytimes.com/2018/04/19/technology/artificial-intelligence-salaries-openai.html |url-status=live }}</ref> where he specialized in [[deep learning]] and [[computer vision]].<ref>{{cite news |last=Metz |first=Cade |date=28 July 2018 |title=The Guy Who Taught AI to 'Remember' Is Launching a Startup |url=https://www.wired.com/2015/07/guy-taught-ai-remember-launching-startup/ |accessdate=9 August 2018 |work=Wired |archive-date=10 August 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180810042437/https://www.wired.com/2015/07/guy-taught-ai-remember-launching-startup/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="qz.com" /><ref name="gs">{{Google scholar id}}</ref> He served as the director of [[artificial intelligence]] and [[Tesla Autopilot|Autopilot]] Vision at [[Tesla, Inc.|Tesla]]. In 2024 he founded Eureka Labs, an AI education platform, whose first course will be LLM101n: Let's Build A Storyteller.<ref>{{cite news |date=16 July 2024 |title=Former OpenAI, Tesla engineer Andrej Karpathy starts AI education platform |url=https://www.reuters.com/technology/artificial-intelligence/former-openai-tesla-engineer-andrej-karpathy-starts-ai-education-platform-2024-07-16/ |accessdate=2 February 2026 |work=Reuters}}</ref> 

==Education and early life==
Karpathy was born in [[Bratislava]], [[Czechoslovakia]] (now Slovakia),<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Slovak, who leads the development of AI at Tesla, is leaving. It was an honor, says Musk – Živé.sk |date=14 July 2022 |url=https://alwaysfreshnews.com/news/world/374155/the-slovak-who-leads-the-development-of-ai-at-tesla-is-leaving-it-was-an-honor-says-musk-zive-sk/ |access-date=2022-07-19 |language=en-US |archive-date=22 May 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230522204001/https://alwaysfreshnews.com/news/world/374155/the-slovak-who-leads-the-development-of-ai-at-tesla-is-leaving-it-was-an-honor-says-musk-zive-sk/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Živé.sk |date=2020-06-25 |title=Šéf AI v Tesle: Rodák zo Slovenska je medzi TOP 35 mladými novátormi |url=https://zive.aktuality.sk/clanok/147462/sef-ai-v-tesle-rodak-zo-slovenska-je-medzi-top-35-mladymi-novatormi/ |access-date=2022-07-19 |website=Živé.sk |language=sk |archive-date=16 November 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231116103853/https://zive.aktuality.sk/clanok/147462/sef-ai-v-tesle-rodak-zo-slovenska-je-medzi-top-35-mladymi-novatormi/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=today |first=newsy |date=2022-03-28 |title=The Slovak, who leads AI in Tesla, left the company for several months. He jokes with Musk about TikTok |url=https://beta.newsy-today.com/the-slovak-who-leads-ai-in-tesla-left-the-company-for-several-months-he-jokes-with-musk-about-tiktok/ |access-date=2022-07-19 |website=Newsy Today |language=en-US }}{{Dead link|date=October 2022 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-06-23 |title=Slovák Andrej Karpathy z Tesly patrí podľa MIT medzi 35 top inovátorov |url=https://www.teslamagazin.sk/andrej-karpathy-tesla-ocenenie-mit/ |access-date=2022-07-19 |website=TeslaMagazin.sk |language=sk-SK |archive-date=20 November 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231120095343/https://www.teslamagazin.sk/andrej-karpathy-tesla-ocenenie-mit/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and moved with his family to [[Toronto]] when he was 15.<ref name=":3">{{Cite news |title=Next Generation Machine Learning - Training Deep Learning Models in a Browser: Andrej Karpathy Interview |url=https://www.datascienceweekly.org/data-scientist-interviews/training-deep-learning-models-browser-andrej-karpathy-interview |access-date=2018-11-12 |work=Data Science Weekly |language=en-US |archive-date=12 November 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181112223544/https://www.datascienceweekly.org/data-scientist-interviews/training-deep-learning-models-browser-andrej-karpathy-interview |url-status=live }}</ref> He completed his Computer Science and Physics bachelor's degrees at [[University of Toronto]] in 2009<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://cs.stanford.edu/people/karpathy/|title=Andrej Karpathy Academic Website|website=cs.stanford.edu|language=en|access-date=2018-11-12|archive-date=2 April 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130402064250/https://cs.stanford.edu/people/karpathy/|url-status=live}}</ref> and his master's degree at [[University of British Columbia]] in 2011,<ref name=":0" /> where he worked on physically simulated figures (for example, a simulated runner or a simulated person in a crowd) with his adviser Michiel van de Panne.

In 2006, Karpathy began posting videos on YouTube on his channel, ''badmephisto''. He garnered fame by posting Rubik's cube tutorials which have been used by famous speedcubers such as [[Feliks Zemdegs]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=I am Feliks Zemdegs, Rubik's cube world record holder. AMA |url=https://bestofama.com/amas/15v3av |access-date=2025-06-24 |website=bestofama.com |archive-date=24 June 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250624114531/https://bestofama.com/amas/15v3av |url-status=live }}</ref> The channel has over 9 million views as of June 2025.

Karpathy received a PhD from [[Stanford University]] in 2015 under the supervision of [[Fei-Fei Li]], focusing on the intersection of [[natural language processing]] and computer vision, and deep learning models suited for this task.<ref name=phd>{{cite thesis|degree=PhD|title=Connecting Images and Natural Language|url=https://searchworks.stanford.edu/view/11849345|website=stanford.edu|year=2016|first=Andrej|last=Karpathy|publisher=Stanford University|archive-date=9 July 2024|access-date=22 July 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240709094555/https://searchworks.stanford.edu/view/11849345|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |date=23 March 2017 |title=Does 'robo-journalism' pose a threat to reporters? |url=https://www.irishtimes.com/business/innovation/does-robo-journalism-pose-a-threat-to-reporters-1.3019182 |accessdate=9 August 2018 |work=The Irish Times |archive-date=2 October 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181002032609/https://www.irishtimes.com/business/innovation/does-robo-journalism-pose-a-threat-to-reporters-1.3019182 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|title=ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge|url=https://doi.org/10.1007/s11263-015-0816-y|journal=International Journal of Computer Vision|date=2015-12-01|issn=1573-1405|pages=211–252|volume=115|issue=3|doi=10.1007/s11263-015-0816-y|language=en|first=Olga|last=Russakovsky|first2=Jia|last2=Deng|first3=Hao|last3=Su|first4=Jonathan|last4=Krause|first5=Sanjeev|last5=Satheesh|first6=Sean|last6=Ma|first7=Zhiheng|last7=Huang|first8=Andrej|last8=Karpathy|first9=Aditya|last9=Khosla|first10=Michael|last10=Bernstein|first11=Alexander C.|last11=Berg|first12=Li|last12=Fei-Fei|url-access=subscription}}</ref>

==Career and research==
He authored and was the primary instructor of the first deep learning course at Stanford, ''CS 231n: Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Stanford University CS231n: Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition |url=http://cs231n.stanford.edu/2015/ |access-date=2022-09-08 |website=cs231n.stanford.edu |archive-date=8 December 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231208150555/http://cs231n.stanford.edu/2015/ |url-status=live }}</ref> The course became one of the largest classes at Stanford, growing from 150 students in 2015 to 750 in 2017.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Andrej Karpathy |url=https://karpathy.ai/ |access-date=2022-09-08 |website=karpathy.ai |archive-date=23 August 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210823180606/https://karpathy.ai/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

Karpathy is a founding member of the artificial intelligence research group [[OpenAI]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-12-12 |title=Introducing OpenAI |url=https://openai.com/blog/introducing-openai/ |access-date=2022-09-08 |website=OpenAI |language=en |archive-date=8 August 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170808104802/https://openai.com/blog/introducing-openai/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Fan |first=Shelly |date=2015-12-20 |title=Inside OpenAI: Will Transparency Protect Us From Artificial Intelligence Run Amok? |url=https://singularityhub.com/2015/12/20/inside-openai-will-transparency-protect-us-from-artificial-intelligence-run-amok/ |access-date=2022-09-08 |website=Singularity Hub |language=en-US |archive-date=21 November 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121160709/https://singularityhub.com/2015/12/20/inside-openai-will-transparency-protect-us-from-artificial-intelligence-run-amok/ |url-status=live }}</ref> where he was a research scientist from 2015 to 2017.<ref name=":1" /> In June 2017 he became Tesla's director of artificial intelligence and reported to [[Elon Musk]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Etherington |first=Darrell |date=2017-06-21 |title=Tesla hires deep learning expert Andrej Karpathy to lead Autopilot vision |url=https://techcrunch.com/2017/06/20/tesla-hires-deep-learning-expert-andrej-karpathy-to-lead-autopilot-vision/ |access-date=2023-11-10 |website=TechCrunch |language=en-US |archive-date=10 November 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231110161130/https://techcrunch.com/2017/06/20/tesla-hires-deep-learning-expert-andrej-karpathy-to-lead-autopilot-vision/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="qz.com">{{cite news |date=21 June 2017 |title=Elon Musk has poached a top mind in AI research—from himself |url=https://qz.com/1011376/elon-musk-poached-andrej-karpathy-from-openai-to-be-teslas-tsla-director-of-artificial-intelligence/ |accessdate=9 August 2018 |work=qz.com |archive-date=10 August 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180810072438/https://qz.com/1011376/elon-musk-poached-andrej-karpathy-from-openai-to-be-teslas-tsla-director-of-artificial-intelligence/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |date=22 June 2017 |title=Tesla hired a top AI expert to lead a critical aspect of Autopilot -- here's what we know |url=https://www.businessinsider.com/tesla-andrej-karpathy-autopilot-vision-2017-6 |access-date=9 August 2018 |work=Business Insider |archive-date=20 September 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180920010923/https://www.businessinsider.com/tesla-andrej-karpathy-autopilot-vision-2017-6 |url-status=live }}</ref> He was named one of [[MIT Technology Review]]'s [[Innovators Under 35]] for 2020.<ref>{{Cite web | title=Andrej Karpathy (Innovators Under 35 2020) | publisher=MIT Technology Review | url=https://www.technologyreview.com/innovator/andrej-karpathy/ | accessdate=8 September 2022 | archive-date=16 August 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230816024049/https://www.technologyreview.com/innovator/andrej-karpathy/ | url-status=live }}</ref> After taking a several-months-long sabbatical from Tesla, he announced he was leaving the company in July 2022.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kolodny |first=Lora |date=2022-07-13 |title=Tesla AI leader Andrej Karpathy announces he's leaving the company |url=https://www.cnbc.com/2022/07/13/tesla-ai-leader-andrej-karpathy-announces-hes-leaving-the-company.html |access-date=2022-07-14 |website=CNBC |language=en |archive-date=26 September 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230926041020/https://www.cnbc.com/2022/07/13/tesla-ai-leader-andrej-karpathy-announces-hes-leaving-the-company.html |url-status=live }}</ref> As of February 2023, he makes YouTube videos on how to create [[artificial neural network]]s.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Andrej Karpathy |url=https://www.youtube.com/@AndrejKarpathy |access-date=2023-02-04 |website=YouTube |archive-date=18 December 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231218031333/https://www.youtube.com/@AndrejKarpathy |url-status=live }}</ref>

On February 9, 2023, Karpathy announced he was returning to OpenAI.<ref>{{Cite tweet |user=karpathy |number=1623476659369443328 |title=Some personal news: I am joining OpenAI (again :)). Like many others both in/out of AI, I am very inspired by the impact of their work and I have personally benefited greatly from it. The future potential is especially exciting; it is a great pleasure to jump back in and build!🪄}}</ref>

A year later on February 13, 2024, an OpenAI spokesperson confirmed that Karpathy had left OpenAI.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theinformation.com/articles/openai-researcher-andrej-karpathy-departs|title=OpenAI Researcher Andrew Karpathy Departs|date=13 February 2024|accessdate=13 February 2024|archive-date=14 February 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240214013240/https://www.theinformation.com/articles/openai-researcher-andrej-karpathy-departs|url-status=live}}</ref> In the same year, he was named one of [[Time (magazine)|Time Magazine]]'s [[Time 100|100 Most Influential People in AI]].<ref>{{Cite magazine |last=Chow |first=Andrew R. |title=Andrej Karpathy: The 100 Most Influential People in AI 2024 |url=https://time.com/7012851/andrej-karpathy/ |access-date=2025-06-26 |magazine=Time |date=2024-09-05 |archive-date=21 June 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250621123152/https://time.com/7012851/andrej-karpathy/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

On July 16, 2024, Karpathy announced on his [[Twitter|X]] account that he started a new AI education company called Eureka Labs.<ref>{{Cite tweet |user=karpathy |number=1813263734707790301 |title=Excited to share that I am starting an AI+Education company called Eureka Labs🪄}}</ref><ref name=":02">{{Cite news |title=OpenAI Co-Founder Andrej Karpathy Announces Eureka Labs, an AI Education Startup |url=https://www.inc.com/ben-sherry/openai-co-founder-andrej-karpathy-announces-eureka-labs-an-ai-education-startup.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240918065604/https://www.inc.com/ben-sherry/openai-co-founder-andrej-karpathy-announces-eureka-labs-an-ai-education-startup.html |archive-date=2024-09-18 |access-date=2024-12-19 |work=Inc.com |language=en}}</ref> Their first product was the AI course, LLM101n.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Eureka Labs |url=https://eurekalabs.ai/ |access-date=2024-12-01 |website=eurekalabs.ai |archive-date=4 December 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241204160358/https://eurekalabs.ai/ |url-status=live }}</ref> He also has a broader educational effort, the "Zero to Hero" series on LLM fundamentals.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Neural Networks: Zero To Hero |url=https://karpathy.ai/zero-to-hero.html |access-date=2025-05-03 |website=karpathy.ai |archive-date=2 May 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250502092542/https://karpathy.ai/zero-to-hero.html |url-status=live }}</ref> The company also advocates for AI teaching assistants, a concept which has been criticized due to data privacy concerns and the removal of personal connection between teacher and student.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Vee |first=Annette |date=2024-08-20 |title=AI pioneers want bots to replace human teachers – here's why that's unlikely |url=https://theconversation.com/ai-pioneers-want-bots-to-replace-human-teachers-heres-why-thats-unlikely-235754#:~:text=OpenAI%20co-founder%20Andrej%20Karpathy,to%20%E2%80%9Cpersonally%20tutor%20all%208 |access-date=2025-05-03 |website=The Conversation |language=en-US |archive-date=14 April 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250414023554/https://theconversation.com/ai-pioneers-want-bots-to-replace-human-teachers-heres-why-thats-unlikely-235754#:~:text=OpenAI%20co-founder%20Andrej%20Karpathy,to%20%E2%80%9Cpersonally%20tutor%20all%208 |url-status=live }}</ref>

In February 2025, Karpathy coined the term [[vibe coding]] to describe how AI tools allow hobbyists to construct apps and websites just by typing prompts.<ref name=":2">{{Cite news |last=Roose |first=Kevin |author-link=Kevin Roose |date=2025-02-27 |title=Not a Coder? With A.I., Just Having an Idea Can Be Enough. |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2025/02/27/technology/personaltech/vibecoding-ai-software-programming.html |access-date=2025-03-03 |work=[[The New York Times]] |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331 |archive-date=3 March 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250303174203/https://www.nytimes.com/2025/02/27/technology/personaltech/vibecoding-ai-software-programming.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

{{Clear}}

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== External links ==
# [https://eurekalabs.ai/ Official website of Eureka Labs]
# # [https://github.com/EurekaLabsAI Official GitHub account of Eureka Labs]
# # # [https://github.com/karpathy Andrej Karpathy] at [[GitHub]]
# # # {{Tesla, Inc.}}
# # #
# # # {{Authority control}}
# # #
# # # {{DEFAULTSORT:Karpathy, Andrej}}
# # # [[Category:Machine learning researchers]]
# # # [[Category:Tesla, Inc. people]]
# # # [[Category:Stanford University alumni]]
# # # [[Category:Living people]]
# # # [[Category:1986 births]]
# # # [[Category:Slovak computer scientists]]
# # # [[Category:Slovak emigrants to Canada]]
# # # [[Category:Canadian computer scientists]]
# # # [[Category:Canadian people of Slovak descent]]
# # # [[Category:University of Toronto alumni]]
# # # [[Category:Scientists from Toronto]]
# # # [[Category:University of British Columbia alumni]]
# # # [[Category:People from Bratislava]]
# # # [[Category:OpenAI people]]
# # # [[Category:Artificial intelligence people]]
# # #
